name: AlgorandClient Package Examples
description: Create runnable examples for the AlgorandClient class from @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils. IMPORTANT: After implementing each example, you MUST run it to verify it works: `npx tsx algorand_client/<example>.ts`. AlgorandClient is the main entry point for all Algorand operations - it provides a unified interface for accounts, transactions, assets, and applications. Most examples require LocalNet to be running.

userStories.title: Infrastructure Setup - Verify Shared Utilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify shared utilities work and set up the algorand_client examples folder structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify examples/shared/utils.ts exists and exports: printHeader(), printStep(), printInfo(), printSuccess(), printError(), formatBytes(), formatHex()
Verify examples/algorand_client folder exists for example files
Verify the root examples/package.json includes @algorandfoundation/algokit-utils dependency
Verify the root examples/tsconfig.json has correct ESM module settings
npm install runs without errors in examples directory
TypeScript compiles without errors: npx tsc --noEmit
Create examples/algorand_client/README.md documenting the examples structure
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required for infrastructure setup

userStories.title: Client Instantiation Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see the different ways to create an AlgorandClient instance, including factory methods for LocalNet, TestNet, MainNet, environment-based configuration, and custom client configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/01-client-instantiation.ts
Demonstrate AlgorandClient.defaultLocalNet() for local development
Demonstrate AlgorandClient.testNet() for TestNet connection
Demonstrate AlgorandClient.mainNet() for MainNet connection
Demonstrate AlgorandClient.fromEnvironment() reading from environment variables (ALGOD_SERVER, ALGOD_TOKEN, ALGOD_PORT, INDEXER_SERVER, INDEXER_TOKEN, INDEXER_PORT)
Demonstrate AlgorandClient.fromConfig() with explicit AlgoConfig object
Demonstrate AlgorandClient.fromClients() with pre-configured algod/indexer/kmd clients
Show how to verify connection by calling algod.status()
Handle connection errors gracefully with helpful error messages
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/01-client-instantiation.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet
userStories.notes: LocalNet required to verify connection works

userStories.title: AlgoAmount Utility Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use the AlgoAmount utility class to work with ALGO and microALGO amounts safely, avoiding floating point precision issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/02-algo-amount.ts
Demonstrate AlgoAmount.Algo() to create amount from ALGO (e.g., AlgoAmount.Algo(1.5))
Demonstrate AlgoAmount.MicroAlgo() to create amount from microALGO
Demonstrate AlgoAmount.Algos() and AlgoAmount.MicroAlgos() aliases
Show .algo property to get amount in ALGO
Show .microAlgo property to get amount in microALGO
Show .toString() for human-readable formatting
Demonstrate arithmetic: addition, subtraction with other AlgoAmount instances
Demonstrate comparison methods or operators
Show how to use AlgoAmount with payment transactions
Demonstrate avoiding floating point issues (e.g., 0.1 + 0.2 precision)
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/02-algo-amount.ts` and verify it completes successfully
userStories.notes: No LocalNet required - pure utility class demonstration

userStories.title: Signer Configuration Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to configure transaction signers on AlgorandClient, including default signers, account-specific signers, and address-specific signers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/03-signer-config.ts
Demonstrate setDefaultSigner() to set a fallback signer for all transactions
Demonstrate setSignerFromAccount() to register a signer from an Account object
Demonstrate setSigner() to register a signer for a specific address
Show how signers are automatically used when sending transactions
Demonstrate registering multiple signers for different accounts
Show how the default signer is used when no specific signer is registered
Create test accounts using algorand.account.random()
Send a payment transaction to verify signer configuration works
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/03-signer-config.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Suggested Params Configuration Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to configure suggested transaction parameters including validity window and params caching for better performance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/04-params-config.ts
Demonstrate setDefaultValidityWindow() to set the number of rounds a transaction is valid for
Show the default validity window and how changing it affects transactions
Demonstrate setSuggestedParamsCache() to enable/disable caching
Demonstrate setSuggestedParamsCacheTimeout() to set cache duration in milliseconds
Show the performance benefit of caching by sending multiple transactions
Demonstrate getSuggestedParams() to manually fetch suggested params
Show how cached params reduce network calls
Explain when to use longer vs shorter validity windows
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/04-params-config.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet
userStories.notes: LocalNet required to fetch suggested params

userStories.title: Account Manager Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use the account manager to create, import, and manage accounts including random accounts, mnemonic-based accounts, KMD wallet accounts, multisig, and logic signatures.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/05-account-manager.ts
Demonstrate algorand.account.random() to generate a new random account
Demonstrate algorand.account.fromMnemonic() to import from 25-word mnemonic
Demonstrate algorand.account.fromEnvironment() to load from env var (ACCOUNT_MNEMONIC)
Demonstrate algorand.account.fromKmd() to get account from KMD wallet (using default LocalNet wallet)
Demonstrate algorand.account.multisig() to create a multisig account
Demonstrate algorand.account.logicsig() to create a logic signature account
Demonstrate algorand.account.rekeyed() to create a rekeyed account reference
Demonstrate algorand.account.getInformation() to fetch account details from network
Demonstrate algorand.account.ensureFunded() to ensure account has minimum balance
Demonstrate algorand.account.ensureFundedFromEnvironment() for dispenser funding
Show account properties: addr, address, publicKey, signer
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/05-account-manager.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet
userStories.notes: LocalNet required for KMD access and account operations

userStories.title: Send Payment Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to send ALGO payment transactions including simple transfers and closing accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/06-send-payment.ts
Create and fund test accounts using account manager
Demonstrate algorand.send.payment() with basic parameters (sender, receiver, amount)
Show using AlgoAmount for the amount parameter
Demonstrate payment with note field
Demonstrate closeRemainderTo to close account and send remaining balance
Show the SendSingleTransactionResult return value: transaction, confirmation, txId
Display transaction ID and confirmed round
Show how to wait for confirmation
Verify balances before and after payment using getInformation()
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/06-send-payment.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Send Asset Operations Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to perform asset operations including creation, configuration, transfer, opt-in, opt-out, freeze, clawback, and destruction.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/07-send-asset-ops.ts
Create and fund test accounts (creator, receiver, frozen account)
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetCreate() to create a new ASA with all parameters (total, decimals, name, unit, url, manager, reserve, freeze, clawback)
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetConfig() to reconfigure an asset
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetOptIn() for receiver to opt into the asset
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetTransfer() to transfer assets between accounts
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetFreeze() to freeze/unfreeze an account's asset holding
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetOptOut() to opt out and close asset holding
Demonstrate algorand.send.assetDestroy() to destroy an asset (all units must be in creator)
Show clawback operation using algorand.send.assetTransfer() with clawbackTarget
Display asset ID and transaction confirmations
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/07-send-asset-ops.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Send App Operations Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to perform application operations including create, update, delete, call, and ABI method calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/08-send-app-ops.ts
Create and fund test accounts
Create a simple TEAL approval and clear program (counter app or simple stateful app)
Demonstrate algorand.send.appCreate() to deploy a new application with global/local schema
Demonstrate algorand.send.appUpdate() to update application code
Demonstrate algorand.send.appCall() for NoOp application calls with args
Demonstrate algorand.send.appCallMethodCall() for ABI method calls (if using ABI)
Demonstrate algorand.send.appOptIn() for account to opt into application
Demonstrate algorand.send.appCloseOut() for account to close out of application
Demonstrate algorand.send.appClearState() to clear local state
Demonstrate algorand.send.appDelete() to delete the application
Show how to pass application arguments, accounts, assets, apps references
Display app ID and transaction confirmations
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/08-send-app-ops.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Create Transaction Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to create unsigned transactions without sending them, for scenarios where I need to inspect, modify, or sign transactions separately.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/09-create-transaction.ts
Create and fund test accounts
Demonstrate algorand.createTransaction.payment() to create unsigned payment transaction
Demonstrate algorand.createTransaction.assetCreate() to create unsigned asset creation
Demonstrate algorand.createTransaction.assetTransfer() to create unsigned asset transfer
Demonstrate algorand.createTransaction.appCall() to create unsigned app call
Show the Transaction object properties: txID(), toByte(), etc.
Demonstrate manually signing a transaction with account.signer
Demonstrate sending a manually signed transaction
Show how to modify transaction fields before signing
Explain use cases: multi-party signing, transaction inspection, custom signing flows
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/09-create-transaction.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet
userStories.notes: LocalNet required for suggested params and account funding

userStories.title: Transaction Composer Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to build atomic transaction groups using the transaction composer, including simulation and execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/10-transaction-composer.ts
Create and fund test accounts
Demonstrate algorand.newGroup() to create a new transaction composer
Add multiple transactions to the group using .addPayment(), .addAssetOptIn(), etc.
Demonstrate chaining: algorand.newGroup().addPayment(...).addPayment(...)
Demonstrate .simulate() to simulate the transaction group before sending
Show simulation results and how to check for failures
Demonstrate .send() to execute the atomic transaction group
Show that all transactions succeed or fail together (atomicity)
Demonstrate adding transactions with different signers
Show the group ID assigned to transactions
Display all transaction IDs and confirmations
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/10-transaction-composer.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Asset Manager Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query asset information and perform bulk opt-in/opt-out operations using the asset manager.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/11-asset-manager.ts
Create and fund test accounts and create a test asset
Demonstrate algorand.asset.getById() to fetch asset information by asset ID
Show asset properties: index, params, created-at-round, deleted flag
Demonstrate algorand.asset.getAccountInformation() to get account's asset holding
Show holding properties: amount, is-frozen, opted-in flag
Demonstrate algorand.asset.bulkOptIn() to opt into multiple assets at once
Demonstrate algorand.asset.bulkOptOut() to opt out of multiple assets at once
Show how bulk operations are more efficient than individual opt-ins
Handle cases where asset doesn't exist or account not opted in
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/11-asset-manager.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: App Manager Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to query application information including global state, local state, box storage, and TEAL compilation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/12-app-manager.ts
Create and fund test accounts and deploy a test application with state and boxes
Demonstrate algorand.app.getById() to fetch application information
Show app properties: id, params, global-state-schema, local-state-schema
Demonstrate algorand.app.getGlobalState() to read global state
Demonstrate algorand.app.getLocalState() to read account's local state
Demonstrate algorand.app.getBoxNames() to list all box names for an app
Demonstrate algorand.app.getBoxValue() to read a specific box value
Demonstrate algorand.app.getBoxValues() to read multiple box values
Demonstrate algorand.app.getBoxValuesFromABIType() to decode typed box values
Demonstrate algorand.app.compileTeal() to compile TEAL source code
Demonstrate algorand.app.compileTealTemplate() to compile TEAL with template variables
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/12-app-manager.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: App Deployer Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to use the app deployer for idempotent application deployment with create, update, and replace strategies.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/13-app-deployer.ts
Create and fund test accounts
Create a simple versioned TEAL application
Demonstrate algorand.appDeployer.deploy() for initial deployment
Show deploy() parameters: name, version, approvalProgram, clearProgram, schema, onUpdate, onSchemaBreak
Demonstrate idempotency: calling deploy() again with same version does nothing
Demonstrate onUpdate: 'update' to update existing app when version changes
Demonstrate onUpdate: 'replace' to delete and recreate app when version changes
Demonstrate onUpdate: 'fail' to fail if app already exists
Demonstrate onSchemaBreak: 'replace' when schema changes require new app
Show deployment metadata stored in app global state
Show how app name is used for idempotent lookups
Display deployment result: app ID, whether created/updated/existing
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/13-app-deployer.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Client Manager Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to access the underlying algod, indexer, and KMD clients through the client manager, and how to get typed app clients.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/14-client-manager.ts
Demonstrate algorand.client.algod to access the raw algod client
Show using algod client directly: status(), getTransactionParams(), etc.
Demonstrate algorand.client.indexer to access the raw indexer client
Show using indexer client: lookupAccountByID(), searchForTransactions(), etc.
Demonstrate algorand.client.kmd to access the raw KMD client
Show using kmd client: listWallets(), listKeys(), etc.
Demonstrate algorand.client.indexerIfPresent() that returns undefined if indexer not configured
Demonstrate algorand.client.getAppClientByCreatorAndName() for typed app client
Demonstrate algorand.client.getAppClientById() for typed app client by ID
Demonstrate algorand.client.getAppFactory() for creating app factory instances
Explain when to use raw clients vs AlgorandClient methods
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/14-client-manager.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet

userStories.title: Error Transformers Example
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to see how to register custom error transformers to enhance error messages and debugging information for failed transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/15-error-transformers.ts
Create and fund test accounts
Explain what error transformers are and why they're useful
Demonstrate algorand.registerErrorTransformer() to add a custom error transformer
Create a transformer that adds source code context to logic errors
Create a transformer that provides user-friendly error messages
Show how transformers receive the error and can return enhanced error
Demonstrate algorand.unregisterErrorTransformer() to remove a transformer
Trigger an intentional error (e.g., insufficient funds, logic rejection) and show enhanced output
Show how multiple transformers can be chained
Show transformer function signature: (error: Error) => Error | undefined
Run the example with `npx tsx algorand_client/15-error-transformers.ts` and verify it works with LocalNet
userStories.notes: LocalNet required for triggering transaction errors

userStories.title: Verification Script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a script to run all AlgorandClient examples and verify they work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create examples/algorand_client/verify-all.sh bash script
Script runs each example in order (01 through 15)
Script reports success/failure for each example
Script displays clear pass/fail status for each example
Script exits with non-zero code if any example fails
All 15 examples pass when running ./verify-all.sh with LocalNet running
Update root examples/verify-all.sh to include algorand_client examples
userStories.notes: LocalNet required - all examples except 02-algo-amount need network access
